NCT02578680.name: KEYNOTE-189
NCT02578680.group: 1L metastatic non-squamous NSCLC, pembrolizumab + pem/plat vs placebo + pem/plat (OS HR)

allOutcomes.title: Overall survival (primary, pembro+chemo vs chemo, HR)

NCT02578680.snippet: Source: Gandhi L et al. NEJM 2018;378:2078-2092 + Rodriguez-Abreu 2024 JCO 5-yr update (KEYNOTE-189).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 616 adults with previously untreated metastatic non-squamous NSCLC without EGFR/ALK alterations randomized 2:1 double-blind to pembrolizumab 200 mg + pemetrexed + platinum (n=410) or placebo + pemetrexed + platinum (n=206) for 4 cycles followed by pembrolizumab or placebo maintenance for up to 35 cycles.

evidence.label: Primary - OS and PFS
evidence.text: Overall survival at median 10.5-month interim: pembro+chemo 12-mo OS 69.2% vs chemo-alone 49.4%; hazard ratio 0.49 (95% CI 0.38-0.64; P<0.001). Updated 5-year OS HR 0.60 (0.50-0.72). PFS HR 0.52 (0.43-0.64). Benefit consistent across PD-L1 <1% / 1-49% / >=50% subgroups.

evidence.label: Secondary - ORR and Safety
evidence.text: ORR: pembro+chemo 47.6% vs chemo 18.9%. Grade >=3 AEs 67.2% vs 65.8%. Immune-related AEs (pneumonitis, nephritis, hepatitis) more common with pembrolizumab. Discontinuation due to AE 14% vs 8%.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by PD-L1. D2 LOW - double-blind placebo-controlled. D3 LOW - 97% follow-up. D4 LOW - BICR for PFS, OS objective. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Has a histologically-confirmed or cytologically confirmed diagnosis of stage IV nonsquamous NSCLC.~* Has confirmation that epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) or anaplastic lymphoma kinase (ALK)-directed therapy is not indicated.~* Has measurable disease.~* Has not received prior systemic treatment for their advanced/metastatic NSCLC.~* Can provide tumor tissue.~* Has a life expectancy of at least 3 months.~* Has a performance status of 0 or 1 on the Eastern Cooperativ...

NCT02775435.name: KEYNOTE-407
NCT02775435.group: 1L metastatic squamous NSCLC, pembrolizumab + carb/tax/nab-pac vs placebo + chemo (OS HR)

allOutcomes.title: Overall survival (primary, pembro+chemo vs chemo, HR)

NCT02775435.snippet: Source: Paz-Ares L et al. NEJM 2018;379:2040-2051 (KEYNOTE-407).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 559 adults with previously untreated metastatic squamous NSCLC randomized 1:1 double-blind to pembrolizumab 200 mg + carboplatin + paclitaxel or nab-paclitaxel (n=278) or placebo + same chemo (n=281) for 4 cycles followed by pembrolizumab or placebo maintenance for up to 35 cycles.

evidence.label: Primary - OS and PFS
evidence.text: Overall survival at median 7.8-month follow-up: median OS 15.9 vs 11.3 months; hazard ratio 0.64 (95% CI 0.49-0.85; P<0.001). PFS HR 0.56 (0.45-0.70). Benefit across all PD-L1 subgroups.

evidence.label: Secondary - ORR and Safety
evidence.text: ORR: pembro+chemo 57.9% vs chemo 38.4%. Grade >=3 AEs 69.8% vs 68.2%. Immune-related AEs elevated with pembrolizumab. Discontinuation due to AE 13% vs 6%.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by PD-L1 and geographic region. D2 LOW - double-blind placebo-controlled. D3 LOW - 97% completion. D4 LOW - BICR for PFS, OS objective. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Has a histologically or cytologically confirmed diagnosis of stage IV (M1a or M1b-American Joint Committee on Cancer [AJCC] 7th edition) squamous NSCLC.~* Has measurable disease based on RECIST 1.1 as determined by the local site investigator/radiology assessment.~* Has not received prior systemic treatment for metastatic NSCLC.~* Has provided tumor tissue from locations not radiated prior to biopsy.~* Has a life expectancy of at least 3 months.~* Has a performance statu...

NCT02366143.name: IMpower150
NCT02366143.group: 1L metastatic non-squamous EGFR/ALK WT NSCLC, atezo + bev + carb + pac vs bev + carb + pac (ABCP vs BCP, OS HR)

allOutcomes.title: Overall survival (primary in EGFR/ALK-WT ITT, ABCP vs BCP, HR)

NCT02366143.snippet: Source: Socinski MA et al. NEJM 2018;378:2288-2301 (IMpower150, ABCP vs BCP arm).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 1,202 adults with previously untreated metastatic non-squamous NSCLC randomized 1:1:1 open-label to atezolizumab + bevacizumab + carboplatin + paclitaxel (ABCP, n=400 total / 356 EGFR/ALK WT), atezolizumab + carboplatin + paclitaxel (ACP, not analysed here), or bevacizumab + carboplatin + paclitaxel (BCP, n=400 total / 336 EGFR/ALK WT). Primary comparison for this app: ABCP vs BCP in EGFR/ALK WT ITT.

evidence.label: Primary - OS in EGFR/ALK-WT ITT
evidence.text: Overall survival: median OS 19.2 months ABCP vs 14.7 months BCP; hazard ratio 0.78 (95% CI 0.64-0.96; P=0.02). Co-primary PFS in EGFR/ALK-WT: HR 0.62 (0.52-0.74).

evidence.label: Secondary - EGFR/ALK Mutant and Liver Metastases Subgroup
evidence.text: EGFR-mutant subgroup OS HR 0.61 (0.29-1.28). Liver-metastasis subgroup OS HR 0.54 (0.33-0.88). Grade >=3 AEs 57% ABCP vs 50% BCP. Immune-related AEs distinctive to atezolizumab arm.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by PD-L1, liver metastases, EGFR/ALK status. D2 LOW - open-label design but OS objective and BICR for PFS. D3 LOW - 97% completion. D4 LOW - BICR for PFS, OS objective. D5 LOW - hierarchical testing pre-specified.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status 0 or 1~* Histologically or cytologically confirmed, Stage IV non-squamous NSCLC~* Participants with no prior treatment for Stage IV non-squamous NSCLC~* Known PD-L1 status as determined by immunohistochemistry assay performed on previously obtained archival tumor tissue or tissue obtained from a biopsy at screening~* Measurable disease as defined by RECIST v1.1~* Adequate hematologic and end organ function~Exclusion Cri...

NCT03164616.name: POSEIDON
NCT03164616.group: 1L metastatic NSCLC, durvalumab + tremelimumab + platinum-doublet vs platinum-doublet (OS HR)

allOutcomes.title: Overall survival (primary, durva+treme+chemo vs chemo, HR)

NCT03164616.snippet: Source: Johnson ML et al. JCO 2023;41:1213-1227 (POSEIDON).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 1,013 adults with previously untreated metastatic NSCLC randomized 1:1:1 open-label to durvalumab + tremelimumab + chemo (n=338), durvalumab + chemo (n=338), or chemo alone (n=337). Primary comparison for this app: durva+treme+chemo vs chemo. Stratified by PD-L1 and histology.

evidence.label: Primary - OS and PFS
evidence.text: OS: median 14.0 vs 11.7 months; HR 0.77 (95% CI 0.65-0.92; P=0.003). PFS HR 0.72 (0.60-0.86). Benefit consistent across squamous and non-squamous.

evidence.label: Secondary - ORR and Safety
evidence.text: ORR: 38.8% vs 24.4%. Grade >=3 AEs 51.8% vs 44.4%. CTLA-4-related IRAEs (colitis, hepatitis, hypophysitis) more prominent than PD-1-monotherapy trials. Discontinuation due to AE 15.5% vs 9.6%.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by PD-L1 and histology. D2 LOW - open-label but OS objective and BICR for PFS. D3 LOW - 95% completion. D4 LOW - BICR for PFS, OS objective. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 130 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~For inclusion in the study, patients should fulfill the following criteria:~1. Aged at least 18 years.~2. Histologically or cytologically documented Stage IV NSCLC.~3. Confirmed tumor PD-L1 status prior to randomization.~4. Patients must have tumors that lack activating EGFR mutations and ALK fusions.~5. title: RapidMeta Oncology | Immunotherapy + Chemotherapy in 1L NSCLC v1.1
